entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: A cereal crop (Pennisetum glaucum L. R.Br.) studied for water stress adaptation.

entities.name: WATER USE TRAITS
entities.description: Traits related to how the plant utilizes water for growth and production.

entities.name: CROP PRODUCTION TRAITS
entities.description: Agronomic traits that determine the overall yield and productivity of the crop.

entities.name: TOLERANCE TO WATER DEFICIT
entities.description: The ability of the plant to maintain productivity under limited water availability.

entities.name: TERMINAL WATER DEFICIT ADAPTATION
entities.description: Adaptation mechanisms specifically for water stress occurring at the end of the growth cycle.

entities.name: LG02-QTL
entities.description: A quantitative trait locus located on linkage group 2 associated with water deficit adaptation.

entities.name: WATER SAVING TRAITS
entities.description: Traits that reduce water consumption to ensure survival and yield under stress.

entities.name: WATER STRESS ADAPTATION
entities.description: The overall process of adjusting plant physiology and morphology to survive water scarcity.

entities.name: AGRONOMIC PERFORMANCE
entities.description: The measurable output and quality of the crop in field conditions.

entities.name: FINE-MAPPING
entities.description: A genetic technique used to narrow down the location of a QTL on a chromosome.

entities.name: POT CULTURE
entities.description: An experimental setup where plants are grown in pots for controlled study.

entities.name: HIGH-THROUGHPUT PHENOTYPING PLATFORM
entities.description: Automated systems used to measure plant traits rapidly and accurately.

entities.name: LYSIMETERS
entities.description: Devices used to measure the amount of water lost from soil through evaporation and transpiration.

entities.name: FIELD
entities.description: The natural agricultural environment where crops are grown.

entities.name: PRINCIPAL COMPONENT ANALYSIS
entities.description: A statistical method used to simplify complex datasets and identify patterns among traits.

entities.name: QTL CO-LOCALIZATION
entities.description: An approach to determine if different traits are controlled by the same or nearby genetic regions.

entities.name: NO/MILD STRESS
entities.description: Environmental conditions with sufficient or slightly limited water availability.

entities.name: SEVERE STRESS
entities.description: Environmental conditions with critical water deficiency.

entities.name: BIOMASS
entities.description: The total mass of living biological organisms in a given area or plant.

entities.name: TILLER PRODUCTION
entities.description: The number of side shoots produced by the plant.

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The amount of crop produced per unit area.

entities.name: PLANT VIGOUR
entities.description: The overall health, growth rate, and robustness of the plant.

entities.name: STAY-GREEN PHENOTYPES
entities.description: The ability of a plant to maintain green leaves and photosynthetic activity during drought.

entities.name: GRAIN FILLING
entities.description: The stage of plant development where nutrients and water are moved into the seeds.
